characters.name: Bee
characters.description:
Bees are Icelandic animals that live in LazyTown. In the episode "Sportafake" one of them annoysMayor Milford Meanswellbefore Milford gets stuck in the tree.
In the episode "Double Trouble", a bee entered Mayor Milford Meanswell's tent and attacked him beforeSportacussaved him. And in the final scene, the bee enteredRobbie Rotten's lair and was about to attack him.
In the episode "Sportacus saves the toys", a swarm of bees collecting sunflower pollen to make their honey

characters.name: Bessie Busybody
characters.description: Bessie BusybodySpeciesHumanGenderFemaleRelativesSportacus(son; only in the original pitch)First appearanceÁfram Latibær!(in all)"Welcome to LazyTown"(in series)Puppeteered byJulie Westwood Bessie Busybodyis a one of the nine main characters ofLazyTown. Bessie's name says it all. She needs to know everyone's business and will go to great lengths to get it. She's usually thwarted by rotten timing or bad luck, but it never discourages her. For instance, she might be eavesdropping on gossip and just as she's about to garner a critical piece of information a loud horn might blare, foiling her.

characters.name: Bobby Rotten
characters.description: BobbySpeciesHumanGenderMaleRelativesTobby RottenFlobby RottenFirst appearance"Robbie's Dream Team"Portrayed byBjörn Thors Bobby Rottenis a minor character inLazyTown. He is one of three goons hired byRobbie Rottento help him with his villainous plans. Bobby appears in the episode "Robbie's Dream Team". Bobby is a member of a trio of actors who perform at weddings, birthday parties, bar mitzvahs, carnivals, mall openings and conferences. He and his two colleagues areTobbyandFlobby Rotten. He’s the most talkative of the bunch and is the de facto leader.

characters.description: The butterfly is a cute insect that appears in every episode. The Butterfly's first first appearance inWelcome to LazyTown

characters.name: Chef Pablo Fantastico
characters.description: Chef Pablo FantasticoSpeciesHumanGenderMaleRelativesNone knownFirst appearanceChef RottenfoodPortrayed byJóhannes Haukur Jóhannesson Chef Pablo Fantasticois a one-time guest character inLazyTown. Chef Pablo Fantastico describes himself as a world famous Mediterranean chef. We see onMayor Meanswell‘s TV that he has his own cooking show, as well as his own cookbook. He has a rather eccentric and outgoing personality, and he’s very passionate about the things he enjoys.

characters.name: Chinese girls
characters.description: The Chinese girls are minor characters who appears in the episodeSportacus saves the Toyswhere they are playing ping-pong until one ofRobbie Rotten's inventions arrives in China, activates itself, and takes their rackets beforeSportacusdeactivates the invention and retrieves and returns the rackets to the Chinese girls

characters.name: Cockatoo
characters.description:
The Cockatoo is a neutral animal bird who appears in the episodeLittle SportacuswhereRobbie Rottenuse his invetion to shrink him in a little before the cockatoo turns into a egg.
In the ending, the cockatoo borns in his egg and says "Mommy" to Robbie Rotten. The Cockatoo is before of the main villain from Rio:Nigel

characters.name: Cow
characters.description: The cow is a animal who isn't appear in the episodes; it's just a decoration on the sign forRobbie Rotten's lair. Robbie also wears a cow costume to try and catchSportacuswith his old inventions. The sound of a cow mooing can also be heard.

characters.name: Dancing Partner
characters.description: The Dancing Parther is a male cake topper who only appears in the episodeWho's Who?.Robbie Rottenuses the cake topper to transform into a wind-up boy who dances withFake Stephanieand realStephaniebefore returning to his actual size (even Fake Stephanie).

characters.name: Dancing Partner
characters.description: The Dancing Parther is a male cake topper who only appears in the episodeWho's Who?.Robbie Rottenuses the cake topper to transform into a wind-up boy who dances withFake Stephanieand realStephaniebefore returning to his actual size (even Fake Stephanie).

characters.name: Fake Stephanie
characters.description: Fake Stephanie is a villainess who only appears in the episodeWho's who?. She's a clone ofStephaniethat Robbie Rotten uses to make a life-size figure of her so she can annoy the kids and make the real Stephanie leave Lazytown. But in the end, the kids figure out that Fake Stephanie is just one of Robbie's toy dolls.

characters.name: Flobby Rotten
characters.description: FlobbySpeciesHumanGenderMaleRelativesBobby RottenTobby RottenFirst appearance"Robbie's Dream Team"Portrayed byBergur Þór Ingólfsson Flobby Rottenis a minor character inLazyTown. He’s one of three goons hired byRobbie Rottento help him with his villainous plans. Flobby appears in the episode "Robbie's Dream Team". Flobby is a member of a trio of actors who perform at weddings, birthday parties, bar mitzvahs, mall openings, carnivals and conferences. He and his two colleagues areBobbyandTobby Rotten. He’s the shortest of the bunch and can’t speak, which is not an impediment for his natural inquisitive and joyful behavior. He can be seen mouthing the words to "We Are Number One" during the musical sequence of "Robbie's Dream Team".

characters.name: French people
characters.description: The French people are minor characters (two boys and a French painter) who only appears in the episode "Sportacus saves the toy" where the two boys play football while the painter paints the Eiffel Tower until one ofRobbie Rotten's inventions arrives in Paris and takes the football away beforeSportacusunplugs it and recovers and returns the ball to the two boys.

characters.name: French people
characters.description: The French people are minor characters (two boys and a French painter) who only appears in the episode "Sportacus saves the toy" where the two boys play football while the painter paints the Eiffel Tower until one ofRobbie Rotten's inventions arrives in Paris and takes the football away beforeSportacusunplugs it and recovers and returns the ball to the two boys.

characters.name: Genie
characters.description: The Genie is a villain who appears in the episodeThe Lazy Genie.Robbie Rottenbought a magic teapot (which he thought was a magic lamp) before Robbie Rotten touched the teapot, a genie appeared. First, Robbie told the genie to undo the Sportcandies, and then the sports equipment (but it reappeared on its own). Finally, Robbie Rotten told the genie he wanted to get rid ofSportacusbefore Robbie disappeared. Sportacus saved the Genie's magic teapot and the genie became friends with the children and Sportacus (except Robbie Rotten).

characters.name: Guest characters
characters.description: These characters have appeared in multiple episodes. The kitten is a young cat who finds itself stuck in a tree often. This cat appears in "Crystal Caper", "Dr. Rottenstein" and "Robbie's Greatest Misses". Roboticus is a villanous robot ordered by Robbie to replace Sportacus as the town hero. He has a major role in "Roboticus" and can also be seen in Robbie's Automatic Wardrobe in "The Lazy Cup" whom he makes to be part of the Spookie Wookies. He is also pictured on Robbie's calendar in "The Holiday Spirit" and in a magazine in "Time to Learn". These characters have appeared in multiple episodes.
characters.personality_section: These characters have appeared in multiple episodes.

characters.name: Haninn
characters.description: HaninnSpeciesRoosterGenderMaleRelativesNone knownFirst appearanceGlanni Glæpur í Latabæ"Welcome to LazyTown"(heard only)Portrayed byGuðmundur Þór Kárason Haninn, known asThe Roosterin English, is a recurring character in theLazyTownfranchise. He is the laziest individual in LazyTown, and his only responsibility is crowing every morning. The Rooster's most prominent appearance is in theLazyTownlive showGlanni Glæpur í Latabæ. When theLazyTownconcept was adapted for television, some of the Rooster's traits were added to the character ofRobbie Rotten. The Rooster does not physically appear in the TV series, but his crowing can be heard frequently throughout the show. Other references to him remain; for example, the symbol of LazyTown is a rooster and theMayor's paper files display a rooster logo. Other, subtler references include a rooster figurehead onRottenbeard's ship and a rooster sculpture in the Mayor's kitchen.

characters.name: Johnny B. Badd
characters.description:
Johnny B. Badd is a minor character who is theMayor Milford Meanswell's favorite musician.
In the episodeRockin' Robbie, Milford shows the children a picture of himself as a young man acting as Johnny B. Badd, wearing violet shoes and holding a banana, beforeRobbie Rottendisguises himself as Johnny B. Badd to grab the trophy and get rid ofSportacusforever.
In the episodeThe Last SportsCandy, Robbie traveled to 1956 (which isn't the correct date), where Johnny B. Badd appears singing while playing guitar and says: I'm having a lot of fun. Thank you very much.

characters.name: Juju
characters.description:
Juju is a male owl who appears in the episode "Haunted Castle" whereSportacusfounds the sound of Juju in his Airship and in the ending's sceneRobbie Rottenhears Juju's sound and runs away from him scary.
In the episode "Who's Who?", Juju appears in a cameo in the exterior of Robbie Rotten's lair Juju's first appearance

characters.name: Mayor Meanswell
characters.description: Mayor MeanswellSpeciesHumanGenderMaleRelativesStephanie(niece)First appearanceÁfram Latibær!(in all)"Welcome to LazyTown"(in series)Puppeteered byDavid Matthew Feldman Mayor Milford Meanswellis a main character inLazyTown. He isStephanie's uncle and the elected head of LazyTown. Mayor Meanswell is a passionate advocate of LazyTown, loving everything about it. He loves that it's the laziest place on Earth. He loves that the town has its very own health hero who is the sworn enemy of laziness. And he loves that the hero has a nemesis! How can one man be in favor of all these contradictory things? Well, he is a politician, after all.

characters.name: Pinocchio
characters.description: Pinocchio is a character who only appears in the episode "New Kid in Town". He is a puppet boy who lives in his book, but he is taken out byRobbie Rottento help him on a mission to lie to the children. First, Pinocchio tells the children (exceptStephanie) that they should eat lollipops for energy and shows them how to lift the rock (but it's just a balloon shaped like a rock by Robbie Rotten) before Pinocchio's nose grows from lying. Pinocchio apologizes to Stephanie and the children for lying and says he wants to go home to his book. When Pinocchio gets his book back and tells them that Robbie tricked him into lying, he says goodbye to the children and goes back to his book. Pinocchio is based on theDisney character of the same name

characters.name: Pinocchio
characters.description: Pinocchio is a character who only appears in the episode "New Kid in Town". Pinocchio is based on theDisney character of the same name

characters.name: Pixel
characters.description:
PixelSpeciesHumanGenderMaleFirst appearanceÁfram Latibær!(in all)"Welcome to LazyTown"(in series)Puppeteered byRonald BinionJulie WestwoodVoiced byKobie Powell(seasons 1-2)Chris Knowings(some season 2 episodes)Ronald Binion(seasons 3-4/Extra) Pixel Hyperbyteis a main character inLazyTown. He’s a computer genius and inventor. He has trouble interacting with other people and coping with problems. He has tendency to invent gadgets that make his life easier, like speeding up his homework, tying his shoelaces, or even playing sports for him. He represents antisocial behavior and his health issue is media moderation. Engaged, earnest and focused, Pixel navigates video games and electronic technology with masterful skill. But all of the hours he's spent logged on have left him ill-prepared for the illogical and uncontrollable forces at play when he hangs with real people. The remote control he carries in his holster can't mute arguments, or change the programming of friends, so he must either retreat to his consistent world of bits and bytes or struggle through the messy moments of friendship. Pixel is the resident computer genius in town. He can do a lot of different things with his computer and all this stuff is pretty well appreciated by the people of Lazy Town. For example, throughout the series, he runs aTV Station, inventsspy gadgetsand helps gather various information with his computer. He really takes pride in his inventions and becomes quite upset if something happens to one of them.
While he can easily understand the logical ways of computing and calculation, Pixel is reclusive and has trouble interacting with people, and understanding the illogical ways of the humans. He loves his friends and his friendship, but he has serious troubles understanding them. He's absent for a good part ofSeason 1(While sinceSeason 2he appears more). Whilst no explanation is given about this fact, it’s assumed that he prefers to spend his time working on his computer than playing outside with his friends.
Pixel's room is the usual meeting place after the playground, it's seem because he has video games. He has a good relationship with all his friends, but sometimesZiggyandStingycan challenge his capacity of understand people. Pixel has little patience, getting fed up quickly when someone it's doing something stupid or annoying.Stephanieis always trying to drag him outside to play, but Pixel usually prefers to stay inside.

Pixel is the resident computer genius in town. He can do a lot of different things with his computer and all this stuff is pretty well appreciated by the people of Lazy Town. For example, throughout the series, he runs aTV Station, inventsspy gadgetsand helps gather various information with his computer. He really takes pride in his inventions and becomes quite upset if something happens to one of them.
While he can easily understand the logical ways of computing and calculation, Pixel is reclusive and has trouble interacting with people, and understanding the illogical ways of the humans. He loves his friends and his friendship, but he has serious troubles understanding them. He's absent for a good part ofSeason 1(While sinceSeason 2he appears more). Whilst no explanation is given about this fact, it’s assumed that he prefers to spend his time working on his computer than playing outside with his friends.
Pixel's room is the usual meeting place after the playground, it's seem because he has video games. He has a good relationship with all his friends, but sometimesZiggyandStingycan challenge his capacity of understand people. Pixel has little patience, getting fed up quickly when someone it's doing something stupid or annoying.Stephanieis always trying to drag him outside to play, but Pixel usually prefers to stay inside.

characters.name: Robbie Rotten
characters.description: Robbie RottenSpeciesHumanGenderMaleFirst appearanceGlanni Glæpur í Latabæ(in all)"Welcome to LazyTown"(in series)Portrayed byStefán Karl Stefánsson† Robbie Rottenis the main antagonist ofLazyTown. Every hero needs a good villain and Sportacus has a doozy in Robbie Rotten. Robbie (or Mr. Rotten to you) can't believe that Sportacus is considered cool by the LazyTown kids, especially since Sportacus isn’t a example of a good LazyTowner! The only thing that can tear Robbie away from snacking and TV in his underground lair is a new, nasty scheme to get rid of his sworn enemy, Sportacus! Robbie's immaturity can be seen in certain childish behavior, fear and lack of development. He’s afraid of the dark, he often jumps when surprised and when confronted or exposed he slinks away with embarrassment. He exhibits a fear of abandonment and he reacts by dreaming up an imaginary friend (the little fly inThe First Day of Summer). Robbie has little to no idea of how to act in social situations. When he attends Ziggy's birthday party inThe Greatest Gifthe’s unsure of how to behave or act around others. Pixel greets him and he simply replies "Whatever." There’s no doubt that Robbie often displays malevolence towards others having a desire to ruin their fun and to teach them a lesson. He’s particularly malevolent towards Sportacus, wanting to drive him away or get rid of him for good. Several times he also tells Stephanie to "go away, little girl." Fortunately his malevolence doesn’t result in physical injury or harm, even despite some of his tricks and rotten plans being downright dangerous. Despite never having been to school, Robbie is shown to be very intelligent. He can create complex inventions rivaled only by that of Pixel, he’s an accomplished chiseler as shown inRottenbeardand able to think up ingenious plans in short amounts of time. Despite all of the above, Robbie does have a soft side, and seemingly a desire to be loved and accepted.  When shown kindness by others he often reacts with appreciation and softness.  When invited to participate he’ll actually participate and in certain situations can even show love and care.
